pr360_status.title: ACCEPT_BACKFILL_DB_DRIFT_2026_0608: update P247G baseline to 2114/22239
pr360_status.what_it_did: Accepted 5 draws inserted during fetcher repair; updated P247G guard to raw=22239 canonical=2114

pr361_status.title: P254A repair fetcher backfill modules
pr361_status.what_it_did: Restored lottery_api/fetcher/* (5 modules); hardened ADD_ON isdigit() guard

pr362_status.title: P254B close fetcher repair governance
pr362_status.what_it_did: Governance closure: incident chain documented, lessons recorded, state returned to HOLD

trigger_path_inventory.notes: Source health-check only.  Calls fetcher.check_source() for each lottery type.  No DB access.
Reads DB draw list + official site.  Returns gap report.  No insert/update/delete.
When insert_if_new=False (default) and dry_run=False: fetches but does NOT insert. When dry_run=True: logs dry_run status only.  Safe path.

trigger_path_inventory.caller: API direct / frontend Fetch-Latest button with Insert checkbox checked
trigger_path_inventory.notes: Calls db_manager.insert_draws() if draw is new.  On success calls _refresh_after_insert() which chains: resolve_pending(dry_run=False) + adjust_all_types(dry_run=False) + apply_learning(dry_run=False).  No server-side authorization token required.

trigger_path_inventory.caller: API direct / frontend Backfill button in dry-run mode
trigger_path_inventory.notes: Passes dry_run=True to BackfillEngine.run().  Engine logs 'dry_run' status per draw but calls no insert.  P254A P254B verification confirmed this path works.

trigger_path_inventory.caller: API direct / frontend Backfill button with confirmed=true and dryRun=false
trigger_path_inventory.notes: BackfillRequest.dry_run defaults to False.  A POST without explicit dry_run=true WILL write.  On any insertion, calls _refresh_after_insert() (resolve_pending + weight_adjuster + learning_integrator, all dry_run=False).  This is the exact path that triggered the P254B incident (5 draws inserted: BIG_LOTTO 115000059, POWER_LOTTO 115000045, DAILY_539 115000136/137/138).

trigger_path_inventory.caller: API direct / AutoFetchManager._loadLog() on page init
trigger_path_inventory.notes: AutoFetchManager constructor calls _loadLog() automatically on instantiation. This is GET only — reads ingest_log.jsonl and returns entries.  No DB write.
Truncates ingest_log.jsonl.  Does not touch the DB.  Audit trail destruction risk.

Frontend soft gate: requires dryRun=true OR confirmed checkbox to be checked. If confirmed checkbox is already checked from a prior session and user clicks backfill without realizing, a non-dry-run write is sent.  Gate is UI-only, not server-enforced.

trigger_path_inventory.caller: Triggered internally on any successful non-dry-run insert (T04 or T06)
trigger_path_inventory.notes: Calls resolve_pending(dry_run=False), adjust_all_types(dry_run=False), and apply_learning(dry_run=False).  These are additional write operations that cascade from a backfill insert.  There is no dry_run pass-through to this chain.
Only calls scheduler.load_data().  No ingest or backfill triggered at startup.

lottery_api/utils/scheduler.py and smart_scheduler.py were grepped for 'auto.*backfill' / 'cron' / 'APScheduler'.  No backfill auto-trigger found. smart_scheduler.py references 'scheduler' for data reload only, not ingest. No background job enqueues /api/ingest/backfill.

trigger_path_inventory.path: Test-only paths: TestClient calls in test_p254a / test_p254b
trigger_path_inventory.notes: test_p254a_fetcher_backfill_repair.py uses FastAPI TestClient with dry_run=True. Tests are isolated and do not write to the canonical DB.

write_capable_paths.caller: API direct / frontend Fetch-Latest button with Insert checkbox checked
write_capable_paths.notes: Calls db_manager.insert_draws() if draw is new.  On success calls _refresh_after_insert() which chains: resolve_pending(dry_run=False) + adjust_all_types(dry_run=False) + apply_learning(dry_run=False).  No server-side authorization token required.

write_capable_paths.caller: API direct / frontend Backfill button with confirmed=true and dryRun=false
write_capable_paths.notes: BackfillRequest.dry_run defaults to False.  A POST without explicit dry_run=true WILL write.  On any insertion, calls _refresh_after_insert() (resolve_pending + weight_adjuster + learning_integrator, all dry_run=False).  This is the exact path that triggered the P254B incident (5 draws inserted: BIG_LOTTO 115000059, POWER_LOTTO 115000045, DAILY_539 115000136/137/138).
Truncates ingest_log.jsonl.  Does not touch the DB.  Audit trail destruction risk.

Frontend soft gate: requires dryRun=true OR confirmed checkbox to be checked. If confirmed checkbox is already checked from a prior session and user clicks backfill without realizing, a non-dry-run write is sent.  Gate is UI-only, not server-enforced.

write_capable_paths.caller: Triggered internally on any successful non-dry-run insert (T04 or T06)
write_capable_paths.notes: Calls resolve_pending(dry_run=False), adjust_all_types(dry_run=False), and apply_learning(dry_run=False).  These are additional write operations that cascade from a backfill insert.  There is no dry_run pass-through to this chain.

auto_trigger_risks.title: BackfillRequest.dry_run defaults to False
auto_trigger_risks.detail: In lottery_api/routes/ingest.py, BackfillRequest.dry_run = False. Any caller that omits dry_run (or sends dry_run=false) will trigger real DB writes. The incident path (T06) went through this default. Mitigation: change default to dry_run=True and require an explicit 'apply=true' flag or a server-side authorization token to perform writes.

auto_trigger_risks.title: Frontend confirmation gate is UI-only (not server-enforced)
auto_trigger_risks.detail: AutoFetchManager._onBackfill() checks 'if (!dryRun && !confirmed) return'. This is enforced only in the browser JavaScript. Any direct HTTP POST to /api/ingest/backfill bypasses this gate entirely. During the P254B incident the frontend sent the POST after the user confirmed, but the server had no additional authorization layer. Mitigation: add a server-side 'apply_confirmed: bool = False' field to BackfillRequest that the server validates before allowing dry_run=False writes.

auto_trigger_risks.title: _refresh_after_insert() cascades multiple write operations with no dry_run option
auto_trigger_risks.detail: After any successful non-dry-run insert (fetch-latest or backfill), _refresh_after_insert() fires resolve_pending(dry_run=False), adjust_all_types(dry_run=False), and apply_learning(dry_run=False). These are additional write paths that execute without any dry_run guard. A single accidental backfill insert triggers 3+ downstream write chains.

auto_trigger_risks.title: CORS allows wildcard origin in development mode
auto_trigger_risks.detail: lottery_api/app.py CORS origins list includes '*'. In development this means any origin can POST to write-capable endpoints. No CORS restriction prevents a cross-origin page from triggering backfill writes. Mitigation: restrict CORS to explicit localhost origins only; remove '*'.

auto_trigger_risks.title: AutoFetchManager._loadLog() auto-fires GET on page load (read-only, not a write risk)
auto_trigger_risks.detail: The AutoFetchManager constructor calls this._loadLog() immediately on instantiation (App.js line 34 creates AutoFetchManager; AutoFetchManager._bindEvents line 79 calls _loadLog). This sends GET /api/ingest/log automatically on every page load. This is READ_ONLY and poses no write risk, but confirms the frontend does auto-contact the ingest API on every page load without user interaction.

auto_trigger_risks.title: No DB pre-write SHA checkpoint before backfill insert
auto_trigger_risks.detail: Before calling db_manager.insert_draws(), neither the route nor the engine creates a SHA fingerprint or DB snapshot. If a partial write occurs (network failure mid-batch), there is no rollback point. The existing DB UNIQUE constraint prevents duplicates but does not protect against partial state. Mitigation: record DB file SHA256 to ingest_log before each write batch.

backfill_endpoint.risk: HIGH — default is write-capable; caller must opt into dry_run=True

fetch_latest_endpoint.risk: LOW — default insert_if_new=False means no write even if dry_run=False
fetch_latest_endpoint.notes: A caller must explicitly set insert_if_new=True to write.

scan_missing_endpoint.risk: NONE — GET endpoint, read-only by design

log_endpoint.risk: NONE — GET endpoint, reads jsonl file only

log_clear_endpoint.risk: LOW — truncates log file only, not DB

add_on_draw_ids.fix_in_p254a: _split() in missing_issue_detector.py now guards with draw.isdigit()
add_on_draw_ids.current_safety: SAFE — non-digit draws return None from _split(); loop skips them

missing_draw_sort.note: _draw_sort_key() in scan() uses try/except int(x) with fallback

recommended_guardrails.title: Change BackfillRequest.dry_run default to True
recommended_guardrails.description: In lottery_api/routes/ingest.py BackfillRequest, change 'dry_run: bool = False' to 'dry_run: bool = True'. All callers must explicitly opt into writes by passing dry_run=False. This is the single highest-impact change to prevent accidental writes.

recommended_guardrails.title: Require explicit server-side confirmation token for write-capable backfill
recommended_guardrails.description: Add an 'apply_confirmed: bool = False' field to BackfillRequest. Server rejects dry_run=False if apply_confirmed is not True. This ensures the server enforces authorization regardless of client behavior. This prevents direct API calls from bypassing the UI gate.

recommended_guardrails.title: UI confirmation dialog before non-dry-run backfill
recommended_guardrails.description: Replace the checkbox confirmation in AutoFetchManager with a modal dialog that explicitly states how many draws will be written and resets after each confirmation so it cannot be left in a pre-confirmed state across test sessions.

recommended_guardrails.title: Audit log entry for every DB write with pre/post row counts
recommended_guardrails.description: Before and after each insert batch, record to ingest_log: pre_count, post_count, and a DB fingerprint. IngestLogger already exists; extend the 'ok' log entry to include counts.

recommended_guardrails.title: Controlled-apply style DB SHA backup before any write batch
recommended_guardrails.description: Before calling db_manager.insert_draws(), compute sha256 of the DB file and log it to the audit log.  Optionally trigger a SQLite online-backup to a timestamped file for rollback capability.

recommended_guardrails.title: Server-side idempotency guard with per-batch insert token
recommended_guardrails.description: Generate a unique idempotency_key per backfill request. Log the key before writes begin.  If the same key is replayed within 60 seconds, reject with 409 Conflict.  This prevents double-submit from network retry.

recommended_guardrails.title: Remove wildcard CORS origin from production configuration
recommended_guardrails.description: lottery_api/app.py origins list includes '*'.  Remove it and enumerate only the allowed local origins.  Gate the wildcard behind an env-var for dev-only mode.

recommended_guardrails.title: Write endpoint disabled unless explicit authorization env-var is set
recommended_guardrails.description: Add INGEST_WRITE_ENABLED=false env-var.  When false, write-capable endpoints return 405 or force dry_run=True.  Set to true only during authorized maintenance windows.
recommended_guardrails.file: lottery_api/routes/ingest.py (new env check at endpoint entry)

incident_retrospective.what_happened: After lottery_api/fetcher/* was deleted (commit 7306264) and then restored (P254A/PR #361), the frontend auto-loaded the ingest page.  The auto-load only fires GET /api/ingest/log (READ_ONLY).  However, during manual validation the user clicked the Backfill button with the confirmation checkbox checked and dry_run=false.  The server had no additional authorization layer, so it accepted the write.  Five draws were inserted: BIG_LOTTO 115000059, POWER_LOTTO 115000045, DAILY_539 115000136/137/138.
incident_retrospective.root_cause_code: BackfillRequest.dry_run defaults to False; no server-side confirm token
incident_retrospective.root_cause_process: No governance gate required dry_run=True during post-repair validation session. Regression check (P247G guard) ran after the insert, not before.

scope_boundaries.already_accepted_baseline_drift: PR #360 — BIG_LOTTO raw=22239, canonical=2114; do NOT reuse stale 22238/2113
scope_boundaries.fetcher_code_repair: PR #361 — 5 modules restored, ADD_ON guard hardened
scope_boundaries.governance_closure: PR #362 — incident chain documented, lessons L_P254_01/02/03 recorded
scope_boundaries.future_ingest_ui_monitoring: NOT in scope — requires separate explicit user authorization
scope_boundaries.strategy_promotion: Out of scope — no active deployable candidate

recommended_next_task: HOLD.  No active deployable candidate.  Future ingest UI or monitoring work requires separate explicit user authorization before proceeding.  Implement guardrails G01+G02 before any future ingest write work is authorized.
final_decision: HOLD.  P255A safety audit is complete.  The write-capable backfill paths have been inventoried and classified.  The primary risk (BackfillRequest.dry_run=False default) is documented with P0 guardrail recommendations (G01+G02).  No DB write occurred in this audit.  Recommended guardrails G01-G08 should be implemented before any future ingest UI or automated monitoring work is authorized.  System returns to WAITING_FOR_USER_AUTHORIZATION.
